017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.beans.*;
023import java.io.*;
024import java.lang.annotation.*;
025import java.lang.reflect.*;
026import java.util.*;
027import java.util.function.*;
028
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.Visibility;
037import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
040
041/**
042 * Context class for classes that use {@link MarshallingContext} objects.
043 *
044 * <p>
045 * This abstraction exists to allow different kinds of subclasses (e.g. JsonSerilalizer, XmlParser...) to share bean context objects since
046 * bean context objects are heavyweight objects that cache metadata about encountered beans.
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
049 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
050 * </ul>
051 */
052@SuppressWarnings({
053   "rawtypes",
054   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_beanContext, ARG_value)
055   "java:S6539" // Central coordination class intentionally aggregates many dependencies.
056})
057public abstract class MarshallingContextable extends Context {
058
059   // Property name constants
060   private static final String PROP_beanContext = "marshallingContext";
061
062   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
063   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
064   private static final String ARG_operation = "operation";
065   private static final String ARG_on = "on";
066   private static final String ARG_properties = "properties";
067   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
068   private static final String ARG_beanClassName = "beanClassName";
069   private static final String ARG_pojoClass = "pojoClass";
070   private static final String ARG_interfaceClass = "interfaceClass";
071   private static final String ARG_implClass = "implClass";
072   private static final String ARG_normalClass = "normalClass";
073   private static final String ARG_swappedClass = "swappedClass";
074   private static final String ARG_swapFunction = "swapFunction";
075   private static final String ARG_unswapFunction = "unswapFunction";
076
077   /**
078    * Builder class.
079    */
080   @SuppressWarnings({
081      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
082   })
083   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends Context.Builder<SELF> {
084
085      private MarshallingContext.Builder bcBuilder;
086      private MarshallingContext bc;
087
088      /**
089       * Constructor.
090       *
091       * All default settings.
092       */
093      protected Builder() {
094         this.bcBuilder = MarshallingContext.create();
095         registerBuilders(bcBuilder);
096      }
097
098      /**
099       * Copy constructor.
100       *
101       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
102       */
103      protected Builder(MarshallingContextable copyFrom) {
104         super(copyFrom);
105         this.bcBuilder = copyFrom.getMarshallingContext().copy();
106         registerBuilders(bcBuilder);
107      }
108
109      /**
110       * Copy constructor.
111       *
112       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
113       */
114      protected Builder(Builder<?> copyFrom) {
115         super(copyFrom);
116         this.bcBuilder = copyFrom.bcBuilder.copy();
117         this.bc = copyFrom.bc;
118         registerBuilders(bcBuilder);
119      }
120
121      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
122      public SELF annotations(Annotation...value) {
123         bcBuilder.annotations(value);
124         super.annotations(value);
125         return self();
126      }
127
128      /**
129       * Minimum bean class visibility.
130       *
131       * <p>
132       * Classes are not considered beans unless they meet the minimum visibility requirements.
133       * For example, if the visibility is <jsf>PUBLIC</jsf> and the bean class is <jk>protected</jk>, then the class
134       * will not be interpreted as a bean class and be serialized as a string.
135       * Use this setting to reduce the visibility requirement.
136       *
137       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
138       * <p class='bjava'>
139       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected class and one field.</jc>
140       *    <jk>protected class</jk> MyBean {
141       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
142       *    }
143       *
144       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that's capable of serializing the class.</jc>
145       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
146       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
147       *       .beanClassVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
148       *       .build();
149       *
150       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo","bar"}</jc>
151       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
152       * </p>
153       *
154       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
155       * <ul>
156       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a non-public bean class to override this setting.
157       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean class to ignore it as a bean.
158       * </ul>
159       *
160       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
161       * <ul>
162       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanClassVisibility()}
163       * </ul>
164       *
165       * @param value
166       *    The new value for this setting.
167       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}.
168       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
169       * @return This object.
170       */
171      public SELF beanClassVisibility(Visibility value) {
172         bcBuilder.beanClassVisibility(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
173         return self();
174      }
175
176      /**
177       * Minimum bean constructor visibility.
178       *
179       * <p>
180       * Only look for constructors with the specified minimum visibility.
181       *
182       * <p>
183       * This setting affects the logic for finding no-arg constructors for bean.  Normally, only <jk>public</jk> no-arg
184       * constructors are used.  Use this setting if you want to reduce the visibility requirement.
185       *
186       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
187       * <p class='bjava'>
188       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected constructor and one field.</jc>
189       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
190       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>;
191       *
192       *       <jk>protected</jk> MyBean() {}
193       *    }
194       *
195       *    <jc>// Create a parser capable of calling the protected constructor.</jc>
196       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = ReaderParser
197       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
198       *       .beanConstructorVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
199       *       .build();
200       *
201       *    <jc>// Use it.</jc>
202       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
203       * </p>
204       *
205       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
206       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotation can also be used to expose a non-public constructor.
207       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean constructor to ignore it.
208       * </ul>
209       *
210       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
211       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanConstructorVisibility()}
212       * </ul>
213       *
214       * @param value
215       *    The new value for this setting.
216       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}.
217       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
218       * @return This object.
219       */
220      public SELF beanConstructorVisibility(Visibility value) {
221         bcBuilder.beanConstructorVisibility(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
222         return self();
223      }
224
225      /**
226       * Returns the inner bean context builder.
227       *
228       * @return The inner bean context builder.
229       */
230      public MarshallingContext.Builder marshallingContext() {
231         return bcBuilder;
232      }
233
234      /**
235       * Specifies an already-instantiated bean context to use.
236       *
237       * <p>
238       * Provides an optimization for cases where serializers and parsers can use an existing
239       * bean context without having to go through <c><jv>marshallingContext</jv>.copy().build()</c>.
240       * An example is {@link MarshallingContext#getBeanToStringSerializer()}.
241       *
242       * @param value The bean context to use.
243       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
244       * @return This object.
245       */
246      public SELF marshallingContext(MarshallingContext value) {
247         bc = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
248         return self();
249      }
250
251      /**
252       * Overrides the bean context builder.
253       *
254       * <p>
255       * Used when sharing bean context builders across multiple context objects.
256       * For example, {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder} uses this to apply common bean settings with the JSON
257       * serializer and parser.
258       *
259       * @param value The new value for this setting.
260       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
261       * @return This object.
262       */
263      public SELF marshallingContext(MarshallingContext.Builder value) {
264         bcBuilder = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
265         return self();
266      }
267
268      /**
269       * Applies an operation to the inner bean context builder.
270       *
271       * @param operation The operation to apply.
272       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
273       * @return This object.
274       */
275      public final Builder marshallingContext(Consumer<MarshallingContext.Builder> operation) {
276         assertArgNotNull(ARG_operation, operation);
277         operation.accept(marshallingContext());
278         return self();
279      }
280
281      /**
282       * Bean dictionary.
283       *
284       * <p>
285       * The list of classes that make up the bean dictionary in this bean context.
286       *
287       * <p>
288       * Values are prepended to the list so that later calls can override classes of earlier calls.
289       *
290       * <p>
291       * A dictionary is a name/class mapping used to find class types during parsing when they cannot be inferred
292       * through reflection.  The names are defined through the {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)} annotation defined
293       * on the bean class.  For example, if a class <c>Foo</c> has a type-name of <js>"myfoo"</js>, then it would end up
294       * serialized as <js>"{_type:'myfoo',...}"</js> in JSON
295       * or <js>"&lt;myfoo&gt;...&lt;/myfoo&gt;"</js> in XML.
296       *
297       * <p>
298       * This setting tells the parsers which classes to look for when resolving <js>"_type"</js> attributes.
299       *
300       * <p>
301       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
302       * <ul>
303       *    <li>Any bean class that specifies a value for {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)}.
304       *    <li>Any subclass of {@link BeanDictionaryList} containing a collection of bean classes with type name annotations.
305       *    <li>Any subclass of {@link BeanDictionaryMap} containing a mapping of type names to classes without type name annotations.
306       *    <li>Any array or collection of the objects above.
307       * </ul>
308       *
309       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
310       * <p class='bjava'>
311       *    <jc>// POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations.</jc>
312       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"foo"</js>)
313       *    <jk>public class</jk> Foo {...}
314       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"bar"</js>)
315       *    <jk>public class</jk> Bar {...}
316       *
317       *    <jc>// Create a parser and tell it which classes to try to resolve.</jc>
318       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
319       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
320       *       .dictionary(Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
321       *       .addBeanTypes()
322       *       .build();
323       *
324       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an indeterminate type.</jc>
325       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
326       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;
327       *    }
328       *
329       *    <jc>// Parse bean.</jc>
330       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{mySimpleField:{_type:'foo',...}}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
331       * </p>
332       *
333       * <p>
334       * Another option is to use the {@link Marshalled#dictionary()} annotation on the POJO class itself:
335       *
336       * <p class='bjava'>
337       *    <jc>// Instead of by parser, define a bean dictionary on a class through an annotation.</jc>
338       *    <jc>// This applies to all properties on this class and all subclasses.</jc>
339       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(dictionary={Foo.<jk>class</jk>,Bar.<jk>class</jk>})
340       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
341       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;  <jc>// May contain Foo or Bar object.</jc>
342       *       <jk>public</jk> Map&lt;String,Object&gt; <jf>myMapField</jf>;  <jc>// May contain Foo or Bar objects.</jc>
343       *    }
344       * </p>
345       *
346       * <p>
347       *    A typical usage is to allow for HTML documents to be parsed back into HTML beans:
348       * <p class='bjava'>
349       *    <jc>// Use the predefined HTML5 bean dictionary which is a BeanDictionaryList.</jc>
350       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = HtmlParser
351       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
352       *       .dictionary(HtmlBeanDictionary.<jk>class</jk>)
353       *       .build();
354       *
355       *    <jc>// Parse an HTML body into HTML beans.</jc>
356       *    Body <jv>body</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"&lt;body&gt;&lt;ul&gt;&lt;li&gt;foo&lt;/li&gt;&lt;li&gt;bar&lt;/li&gt;&lt;/ul&gt;"</js>, Body.<jk>class</jk>);
357       * </p>
358       *
359       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
360       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#dictionary()}
361       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledProp#dictionary()}
362       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#dictionary()}
363       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#dictionary_replace()}
364       * </ul>
365       *
366       * @param values
367       *    The values to add to this setting.
368       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
369       * @return This object.
370       */
371      public SELF beanDictionary(Class<?>...values) {
372         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
373         bcBuilder.beanDictionary(values);
374         return self();
375      }
376
377      /**
378       * Minimum bean field visibility.
379       *
380       * <p>
381       * Only look for bean fields with the specified minimum visibility.
382       *
383       * <p>
384       * This affects which fields on a bean class are considered bean properties.  Normally only <jk>public</jk> fields are considered.
385       * Use this setting if you want to reduce the visibility requirement.
386       *
387       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
388       * <p class='bjava'>
389       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected field.</jc>
390       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
391       *       <jk>protected</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
392       *    }
393       *
394       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that recognizes the protected field.</jc>
395       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
396       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
397       *       .beanFieldVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
398       *       .build();
399       *
400       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"bar"}</jc>
401       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
402       * </p>
403       *
404       * <p>
405       * Bean fields can be ignored as properties entirely by setting the value to {@link Visibility#NONE}
406       *
407       * <p class='bjava'>
408       *    <jc>// Disable using fields as properties entirely.</jc>
409       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
410       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
411       *       .beanFieldVisibility(<jsf>NONE</jsf>)
412       *       .build();
413       * </p>
414       *
415       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
416       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used to expose a non-public field.
417       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean field to ignore it as a bean property.
418       * </ul>
419       *
420       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
421       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanFieldVisibility()}
422       * </ul>
423       *
424       * @param value
425       *    The new value for this setting.
426       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}.
427       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
428       * @return This object.
429       */
430      public SELF beanFieldVisibility(Visibility value) {
431         bcBuilder.beanFieldVisibility(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
432         return self();
433      }
434
435      /**
436       * Bean interceptor.
437       *
438       * <p>
439       * Bean interceptors can be used to intercept calls to getters and setters and alter their values in transit.
440       *
441       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
442       * <p class='bjava'>
443       *    <jc>// Interceptor that strips out sensitive information.</jc>
444       *    <jk>public class</jk> AddressInterceptor <jk>extends</jk> BeanInterceptor&lt;Address&gt; {
445       *
446       *       <jk>public</jk> Object readProperty(Address <jv>bean</jv>, String <jv>name</jv>, Object <jv>value</jv>) {
447       *          <jk>if</jk> (<js>"taxInfo"</js>.equals(<jv>name</jv>))
448       *             <jk>return</jk> <js>"redacted"</js>;
449       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>value</jv>;
450       *       }
451       *
452       *       <jk>public</jk> Object writeProperty(Address <jv>bean</jv>, String <jv>name</jv>, Object <jv>value</jv>) {
453       *          <jk>if</jk> (<js>"taxInfo"</js>.equals(<jv>name</jv>) &amp;&amp; <js>"redacted"</js>.equals(<jv>value</jv>))
454       *             <jk>return</jk> TaxInfoUtils.<jsm>lookup</jsm>(<jv>bean</jv>.getStreet(), <jv>bean</jv>.getCity(), <jv>bean</jv>.getState());
455       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>value</jv>;
456       *       }
457       *    }
458       *
459       *    <jc>// Our bean class.</jc>
460       *    <jk>public class</jk> Address {
461       *       <jk>public</jk> String getTaxInfo() {...}
462       *       <jk>public void</jk> setTaxInfo(String <jv>value</jv>) {...}
463       *    }
464       *
465       *    <jc>// Register filter on serializer or parser.</jc>
466       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
467       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
468       *       .beanInterceptor(Address.<jk>class</jk>, AddressInterceptor.<jk>class</jk>)
469       *       .build();
470       *
471       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"taxInfo":"redacted"}</jc>
472       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> Address());
473       * </p>
474       *
475       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
476       *    <li class='jc'>{@link BeanInterceptor}
477       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#interceptor() @Marshalled(interceptor)}
478       * </ul>
479       *
480       * @param on The bean that the filter applies to.
481       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
482       * @param value
483       *    The new value for this setting.
484       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
485       * @return This object.
486       */
487      public SELF beanInterceptor(Class<?> on, Class<? extends BeanInterceptor<?>> value) {
488         bcBuilder.beanInterceptor(assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on), assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
489         return self();
490      }
491
492      /**
493       * BeanMap.put() returns old property value.
494       *
495       * <p>
496       * When enabled, then the {@link BeanMap#put(String,Object) BeanMap.put()} method will return old property
497       * values.  Otherwise, it returns <jk>null</jk>.
498       *
499       * <p>
500       * Disabled by default because it introduces a slight performance penalty during serialization.
501       *
502       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
503       * <p class='bjava'>
504       *    <jc>// Create a context that creates BeanMaps with normal put() behavior.</jc>
505       *    MarshallingContext <jv>context</jv> = MarshallingContext
506       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
507       *       .beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()
508       *       .build();
509       *
510       *    BeanMap&lt;MyBean&gt; <jv>beanMap</jv> = <jv>context</jv>.createSession().toBeanMap(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
511       *    <jv>beanMap</jv>.put(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>);
512       *    Object <jv>oldValue</jv> = <jv>beanMap</jv>.put(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"baz"</js>);  <jc>// oldValue == "bar"</jc>
513       * </p>
514       *
515       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
516       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()}
517       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()}
518       * </ul>
519       *
520       * @return This object.
521       */
522      public SELF beanMapPutReturnsOldValue() {
523         bcBuilder.beanMapPutReturnsOldValue();
524         return self();
525      }
526
527      /**
528       * Minimum bean method visibility.
529       *
530       * <p>
531       * Only look for bean methods with the specified minimum visibility.
532       *
533       * <p>
534       * This affects which methods are detected as getters and setters on a bean class. Normally only <jk>public</jk> getters and setters are considered.
535       * Use this setting if you want to reduce the visibility requirement.
536       *
537       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
538       * <p class='bjava'>
539       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected getter.</jc>
540       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
541       *       <jk>public</jk> String getFoo() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"foo"</js>; }
542       *       <jk>protected</jk> String getBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>; }
543       *    }
544       *
545       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that looks for protected getters and setters.</jc>
546       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
547       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
548       *       .beanMethodVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
549       *       .build();
550       *
551       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
552       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
553       * </p>
554       *
555       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
556       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used to expose a non-public method.
557       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean getter/setter to ignore it as a bean property.
558       * </ul>
559       *
560       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
561       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanMethodVisibility()}
562       * </ul>
563       *
564       * @param value
565       *    The new value for this setting.
566       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}
567       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
568       * @return This object.
569       */
570      public SELF beanMethodVisibility(Visibility value) {
571         bcBuilder.beanMethodVisibility(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
572         return self();
573      }
574
575      /**
576       * Bean property includes.
577       *
578       * <p>
579       * Specifies the set and order of names of properties associated with the bean class.
580       *
581       * <p>
582       * For example, <c>beanProperties(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)</c> means only serialize the <c>foo</c> and
583       * <c>bar</c> properties on the specified bean.  Likewise, parsing will ignore any bean properties not specified
584       * and either throw an exception or silently ignore them depending on whether {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
585       * has been called.
586       *
587       * <p>
588       * This value is entirely optional if you simply want to expose all the getters and public fields on
589       * a class as bean properties.  However, it's useful if you want certain getters to be ignored or you want the properties to be
590       * serialized in a particular order.  Note that on IBM JREs, the property order is the same as the order in the source code,
591       * whereas on Oracle JREs, the order is entirely random.
592       *
593       * <p>
594       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
595       *
596       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
597       * <p class='bjava'>
598       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
599       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
600       *       <jk>public</jk> String
601       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
602       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
603       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
604       *    }
605       *
606       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that includes only the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
607       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
608       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
609       *       .beanProperties(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)
610       *       .build();
611       *
612       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
613       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
614       * </p>
615       *
616       * <p>
617       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
618       * <p class='bjava'>
619       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).properties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
620       * </p>
621       *
622       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
623       *    <li class='jm'>{@link BeanType#properties()}/{@link BeanType#p()} - On an annotation on the bean class itself.
624       * </ul>
625       *
626       * @param beanClass The bean class.
627       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
628       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
629       * @return This object.
630       */
631      public SELF beanProperties(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
632         bcBuilder.beanProperties(beanClass, assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
633         return self();
634      }
635
636      /**
637       * Bean property includes.
638       *
639       * <p>
640       * Specifies the set and order of names of properties associated with bean classes.
641       *
642       * <p>
643       * For example, <c>beanProperties(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>))</c> means only serialize the <c>foo</c> and
644       * <c>bar</c> properties on the specified bean.  Likewise, parsing will ignore any bean properties not specified
645       * and either throw an exception or silently ignore them depending on whether {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
646       * has been called.
647       *
648       * <p>
649       * This value is entirely optional if you simply want to expose all the getters and public fields on
650       * a class as bean properties.  However, it's useful if you want certain getters to be ignored or you want the properties to be
651       * serialized in a particular order.  Note that on IBM JREs, the property order is the same as the order in the source code,
652       * whereas on Oracle JREs, the order is entirely random.
653       *
654       * <p>
655       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
656       *
657       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
658       * <p class='bjava'>
659       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
660       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
661       *       <jk>public</jk> String
662       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
663       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
664       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
665       *    }
666       *
667       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that includes only the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
668       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
669       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
670       *       .beanProperties(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>))
671       *       .build();
672       *
673       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
674       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
675       * </p>
676       *
677       * <p>
678       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
679       * <p class='bjava'>
680       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).properties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
681       * </p>
682       *
683       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
684       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#properties()} / {@link BeanType#p()}- On an annotation on the bean class itself.
685       * </ul>
686       *
687       * @param values
688       *    The values to add to this builder.
689       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
690       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
691       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
692       * @return This object.
693       */
694      public SELF beanProperties(Map<String,Object> values) {
695         bcBuilder.beanProperties(assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values));
696         return self();
697      }
698
699      /**
700       * Bean property includes.
701       *
702       * <p>
703       * Specifies the set and order of names of properties associated with the bean class.
704       *
705       * <p>
706       * For example, <c>beanProperties(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)</c> means only serialize the <c>foo</c> and
707       * <c>bar</c> properties on the specified bean.  Likewise, parsing will ignore any bean properties not specified
708       * and either throw an exception or silently ignore them depending on whether {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
709       * has been called.
710       *
711       * <p>
712       * This value is entirely optional if you simply want to expose all the getters and public fields on
713       * a class as bean properties.  However, it's useful if you want certain getters to be ignored or you want the properties to be
714       * serialized in a particular order.  Note that on IBM JREs, the property order is the same as the order in the source code,
715       * whereas on Oracle JREs, the order is entirely random.
716       *
717       * <p>
718       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
719       *
720       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
721       * <p class='bjava'>
722       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
723       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
724       *       <jk>public</jk> String
725       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
726       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
727       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
728       *    }
729       *
730       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that includes only the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
731       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
732       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
733       *       .beanProperties(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)
734       *       .build();
735       *
736       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
737       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
738       * </p>
739       *
740       * <p>
741       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
742       * <p class='bjava'>
743       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).properties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
744       * </p>
745       *
746       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
747       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#properties()} / {@link BeanType#p()} - On an annotation on the bean class itself.
748       * </ul>
749       *
750       * @param beanClassName
751       *    The bean class name.
752       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
753       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
754       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
755       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
756       * @return This object.
757       */
758      public SELF beanProperties(String beanClassName, String properties) {
759         bcBuilder.beanProperties(assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName), assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
760         return self();
761      }
762
763      /**
764       * Bean property excludes.
765       *
766       * <p>
767       * Specifies to exclude the specified list of properties for the specified bean class.
768       *
769       * <p>
770       * Same as {@link #beanProperties(Class, String)} except you specify a list of bean property names that you want to exclude from
771       * serialization.
772       *
773       * <p>
774       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
775       *
776       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
777       * <p class='bjava'>
778       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
779       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
780       *       <jk>public</jk> String
781       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
782       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
783       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
784       *    }
785       *
786       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the "bar" and "baz" properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
787       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
788       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
789       *       .beanPropertiesExcludes(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
790       *       .build();
791       *
792       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
793       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
794       * </p>
795       *
796       * <p>
797       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
798       * <p class='bjava'>
799       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).excludeProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
800       * </p>
801       *
802       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
803       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#excludeProperties()} / {@link BeanType#xp()}
804       * </ul>
805       *
806       * @param beanClass The bean class.
807       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
808       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
809       * @return This object.
810       */
811      public SELF beanPropertiesExcludes(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
812         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesExcludes(beanClass, assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
813         return self();
814      }
815
816      /**
817       * Bean property excludes.
818       *
819       * <p>
820       * Specifies to exclude the specified list of properties for the specified bean classes.
821       *
822       * <p>
823       * Same as {@link #beanProperties(Map)} except you specify a list of bean property names that you want to exclude from
824       * serialization.
825       *
826       * <p>
827       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
828       *
829       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
830       * <p class='bjava'>
831       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
832       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
833       *       <jk>public</jk> String
834       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
835       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
836       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
837       *    }
838       *
839       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the "bar" and "baz" properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
840       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
841       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
842       *       .beanPropertiesExcludes(AMap.of(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
843       *       .build();
844       *
845       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
846       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
847       * </p>
848       *
849       * <p>
850       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
851       * <p class='bjava'>
852       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).excludeProperties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
853       * </p>
854       *
855       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
856       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#excludeProperties()} / {@link BeanType#xp()}
857       * </ul>
858       *
859       * @param values
860       *    The values to add to this builder.
861       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
862       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
863       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
864       * @return This object.
865       */
866      public SELF beanPropertiesExcludes(Map<String,Object> values) {
867         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesExcludes(assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values));
868         return self();
869      }
870
871      /**
872       * Bean property excludes.
873       *
874       * <p>
875       * Specifies to exclude the specified list of properties for the specified bean class.
876       *
877       * <p>
878       * Same as {@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(String, String)} except you specify a list of bean property names that you want to exclude from
879       * serialization.
880       *
881       * <p>
882       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
883       *
884       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
885       * <p class='bjava'>
886       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
887       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
888       *       <jk>public</jk> String
889       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
890       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
891       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
892       *    }
893       *
894       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the "bar" and "baz" properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
895       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
896       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
897       *       .beanPropertiesExcludes(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
898       *       .build();
899       *
900       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
901       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
902       * </p>
903       *
904       * <p>
905       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
906       * <p class='bjava'>
907       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).excludeProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
908       * </p>
909       *
910       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
911       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#excludeProperties()} / {@link BeanType#xp()}
912       * </ul>
913       *
914       * @param beanClassName
915       *    The bean class name.
916       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all bean classes.
917       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
918       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
919       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
920       * @return This object.
921       */
922      public SELF beanPropertiesExcludes(String beanClassName, String properties) {
923         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesExcludes(assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName), assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
924         return self();
925      }
926
927      /**
928       * Read-only bean properties.
929       *
930       * <p>
931       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are read-only despite having valid getters.
932       * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
933       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
934       * for both serializers and parsers.
935       *
936       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
937       * <p class='bjava'>
938       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
939       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
940       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
941       *    }
942       *
943       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with read-only property settings.</jc>
944       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
945       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
946       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
947       *       .build();
948       *
949       *    <jc>// All 3 properties will be serialized.</jc>
950       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
951       *
952       *    <jc>// Create a parser with read-only property settings.</jc>
953       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
954       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
955       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
956       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
957       *       .build();
958       *
959       *    <jc>// Parser ignores bar and baz properties.</jc>
960       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
961       * </p>
962       *
963       * <p>
964       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
965       * <p class='bjava'>
966       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).readOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
967       * </p>
968       *
969       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
970       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#readOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#ro()}
971       * </ul>
972       *
973       * @param beanClass The bean class.
974       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
975       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
976       * @return This object.
977       */
978      public SELF beanPropertiesReadOnly(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
979         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesReadOnly(beanClass, assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
980         return self();
981      }
982
983      /**
984       * Read-only bean properties.
985       *
986       * <p>
987       * Specifies one or more properties on beans that are read-only despite having valid getters.
988       * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
989       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
990       * for both serializers and parsers.
991       *
992       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
993       * <p class='bjava'>
994       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
995       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
996       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
997       *    }
998       *
999       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with read-only property settings.</jc>
1000       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1001       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1002       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1003       *       .build();
1004       *
1005       *    <jc>// All 3 properties will be serialized.</jc>
1006       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1007       *
1008       *    <jc>// Create a parser with read-only property settings.</jc>
1009       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1010       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1011       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1012       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
1013       *       .build();
1014       *
1015       *    <jc>// Parser ignores bar and baz properties.</jc>
1016       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1017       * </p>
1018       *
1019       * <p>
1020       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
1021       * <p class='bjava'>
1022       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).readOnlyProperties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
1023       * </p>
1024       *
1025       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1026       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#readOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#ro()}
1027       * </ul>
1028       *
1029       * @param values
1030       *    The values to add to this builder.
1031       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1032       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
1033       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1034       * @return This object.
1035       */
1036      public SELF beanPropertiesReadOnly(Map<String,Object> values) {
1037         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesReadOnly(assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values));
1038         return self();
1039      }
1040
1041      /**
1042       * Read-only bean properties.
1043       *
1044       * <p>
1045       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are read-only despite having valid getters.
1046       * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
1047       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1048       * for both serializers and parsers.
1049       *
1050       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1051       * <p class='bjava'>
1052       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1053       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1054       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1055       *    }
1056       *
1057       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with read-only property settings.</jc>
1058       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1059       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1060       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1061       *       .build();
1062       *
1063       *    <jc>// All 3 properties will be serialized.</jc>
1064       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1065       *
1066       *    <jc>// Create a parser with read-only property settings.</jc>
1067       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1068       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1069       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1070       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
1071       *       .build();
1072       *
1073       *    <jc>// Parser ignores bar and baz properties.</jc>
1074       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1075       * </p>
1076       *
1077       * <p>
1078       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1079       * <p class='bjava'>
1080       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).readOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1081       * </p>
1082       *
1083       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1084       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#readOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#ro()}
1085       * </ul>
1086       *
1087       * @param beanClassName
1088       *    The bean class name.
1089       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all bean classes.
1090       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1091       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1092       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1093       * @return This object.
1094       */
1095      public SELF beanPropertiesReadOnly(String beanClassName, String properties) {
1096         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesReadOnly(assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName), assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
1097         return self();
1098      }
1099
1100      /**
1101       * Write-only bean properties.
1102       *
1103       * <p>
1104       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
1105       * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
1106       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1107       * for both serializers and parsers.
1108       *
1109       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1110       * <p class='bjava'>
1111       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1112       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1113       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1114       *    }
1115       *
1116       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with write-only property settings.</jc>
1117       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1118       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1119       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1120       *       .build();
1121       *
1122       *    <jc>// Only foo will be serialized.</jc>
1123       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1124       *
1125       *    <jc>// Create a parser with write-only property settings.</jc>
1126       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1127       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1128       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1129       *       .build();
1130       *
1131       *    <jc>// Parser parses all 3 properties.</jc>
1132       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1133       * </p>
1134       *
1135       * <p>
1136       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1137       * <p class='bjava'>
1138       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).writeOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1139       * </p>
1140       *
1141       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1142       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#writeOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#wo()}
1143       * </ul>
1144       *
1145       * @param beanClass The bean class.
1146       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1147       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1148       * @return This object.
1149       */
1150      public SELF beanPropertiesWriteOnly(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
1151         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesWriteOnly(beanClass, assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
1152         return self();
1153      }
1154
1155      /**
1156       * Write-only bean properties.
1157       *
1158       * <p>
1159       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
1160       * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
1161       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1162       * for both serializers and parsers.
1163       *
1164       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1165       * <p class='bjava'>
1166       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1167       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1168       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1169       *    }
1170       *
1171       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with write-only property settings.</jc>
1172       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1173       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1174       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1175       *       .build();
1176       *
1177       *    <jc>// Only foo will be serialized.</jc>
1178       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1179       *
1180       *    <jc>// Create a parser with write-only property settings.</jc>
1181       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1182       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1183       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1184       *       .build();
1185       *
1186       *    <jc>// Parser parses all 3 properties.</jc>
1187       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1188       * </p>
1189       *
1190       * <p>
1191       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
1192       * <p class='bjava'>
1193       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).writeOnlyProperties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
1194       * </p>
1195       *
1196       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1197       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#writeOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#wo()}
1198       * </ul>
1199       *
1200       * @param values
1201       *    The values to add to this builder.
1202       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1203       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
1204       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1205       * @return This object.
1206       */
1207      public SELF beanPropertiesWriteOnly(Map<String,Object> values) {
1208         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesWriteOnly(assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values));
1209         return self();
1210      }
1211
1212      /**
1213       * Write-only bean properties.
1214       *
1215       * <p>
1216       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
1217       * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
1218       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1219       * for both serializers and parsers.
1220       *
1221       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1222       * <p class='bjava'>
1223       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1224       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1225       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1226       *    }
1227       *
1228       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with write-only property settings.</jc>
1229       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1230       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1231       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1232       *       .build();
1233       *
1234       *    <jc>// Only foo will be serialized.</jc>
1235       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1236       *
1237       *    <jc>// Create a parser with write-only property settings.</jc>
1238       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1239       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1240       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1241       *       .build();
1242       *
1243       *    <jc>// Parser parses all 3 properties.</jc>
1244       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1245       * </p>
1246       *
1247       * <p>
1248       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1249       * <p class='bjava'>
1250       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).writeOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1251       * </p>
1252       *
1253       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1254       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#writeOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#wo()}
1255       * </ul>
1256       *
1257       * @param beanClassName
1258       *    The bean class name.
1259       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all bean classes.
1260       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1261       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1262       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1263       * @return This object.
1264       */
1265      public SELF beanPropertiesWriteOnly(String beanClassName, String properties) {
1266         bcBuilder.beanPropertiesWriteOnly(assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName), assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties));
1267         return self();
1268      }
1269
1270      /**
1271       * Beans require no-arg constructors.
1272       *
1273       * <p>
1274       * When enabled, a Java class must implement a default no-arg constructor to be considered a bean.
1275       * Otherwise, the bean will be serialized as a string using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
1276       *
1277       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1278       * <p class='bjava'>
1279       *    <jc>// A bean without a no-arg constructor.</jc>
1280       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1281       *
1282       *       <jc>// A property method.</jc>
1283       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
1284       *
1285       *       <jc>// A no-arg constructor</jc>
1286       *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean(String <jv>foo</jv>) {
1287       *          <jk>this</jk>.<jf>foo</jf> = <jv>foo</jv>;
1288       *       }
1289       *
1290       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1291       *       <jk>public</jk> String toString() {
1292       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>;
1293       *       }
1294       *    }
1295       *
1296       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores beans without default constructors.</jc>
1297       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1298       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1299       *       .beansRequireDefaultConstructor()
1300       *       .build();
1301       *
1302       *    <jc>// Produces:  "bar"</jc>
1303       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1304       * </p>
1305       *
1306       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1307       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a bean class to override this setting.
1308       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a class to ignore it as a bean.
1309       * </ul>
1310       *
1311       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1312       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beansRequireDefaultConstructor()}
1313       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beansRequireDefaultConstructor()}
1314       * </ul>
1315       *
1316       * @return This object.
1317       */
1318      public SELF beansRequireDefaultConstructor() {
1319         bcBuilder.beansRequireDefaultConstructor();
1320         return self();
1321      }
1322
1323      /**
1324       * Beans require Serializable interface.
1325       *
1326       * <p>
1327       * When enabled, a Java class must implement the {@link Serializable} interface to be considered a bean.
1328       * Otherwise, the bean will be serialized as a string using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
1329       *
1330       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1331       * <p class='bjava'>
1332       *    <jc>// A bean without a Serializable interface.</jc>
1333       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1334       *
1335       *       <jc>// A property method.</jc>
1336       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
1337       *
1338       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1339       *       <jk>public</jk> String toString() {
1340       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>;
1341       *       }
1342       *    }
1343       *
1344       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores beans not implementing Serializable.</jc>
1345       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1346       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1347       *       .beansRequireSerializable()
1348       *       .build();
1349       *
1350       *    <jc>// Produces:  "bar"</jc>
1351       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1352       * </p>
1353       *
1354       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1355       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a bean class to override this setting.
1356       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a class to ignore it as a bean.
1357       * </ul>
1358       *
1359       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1360       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beansRequireSerializable()}
1361       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beansRequireSerializable()}
1362       * </ul>
1363       *
1364       * @return This object.
1365       */
1366      public SELF beansRequireSerializable() {
1367         bcBuilder.beansRequireSerializable();
1368         return self();
1369      }
1370
1371      /**
1372       * Beans require setters for getters.
1373       *
1374       * <p>
1375       * When enabled, ignore read-only properties (properties with getters but not setters).
1376       *
1377       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1378       * <p class='bjava'>
1379       *    <jc>// A bean without a Serializable interface.</jc>
1380       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1381       *
1382       *       <jc>// A read/write property.</jc>
1383       *       <jk>public</jk> String getFoo() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"foo"</js>; }
1384       *       <jk>public void</jk> setFoo(String <jv>foo</jv>) { ... }
1385       *
1386       *       <jc>// A read-only property.</jc>
1387       *       <jk>public</jk> String getBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>; }
1388       *    }
1389       *
1390       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores bean properties without setters.</jc>
1391       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1392       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1393       *       .beansRequireSettersForGetters()
1394       *       .build();
1395       *
1396       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
1397       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1398       * </p>
1399       *
1400       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1401       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can be used on the getter to override this setting.
1402       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on getters to ignore them as bean properties.
1403       * </ul>
1404       *
1405       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1406       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beansRequireSettersForGetters()}
1407       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beansRequireSettersForGetters()}
1408       * </ul>
1409       *
1410       * @return This object.
1411       */
1412      public SELF beansRequireSettersForGetters() {
1413         bcBuilder.beansRequireSettersForGetters();
1414         return self();
1415      }
1416
1417      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
1418      public abstract SELF copy();
1419
1420      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
1421      public SELF debug() {
1422         bcBuilder.debug();
1423         super.debug();
1424         return self();
1425      }
1426
1427      /**
1428       * Bean dictionary.
1429       *
1430       * <p>
1431       * This is identical to {@link #beanDictionary(Class...)}, but specifies a dictionary within the context of
1432       * a single class as opposed to globally.
1433       *
1434       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1435       * <p class='bjava'>
1436       *    <jc>// POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations.</jc>
1437       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"foo"</js>)
1438       *    <jk>public class</jk> Foo {...}
1439       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"bar"</js>)
1440       *    <jk>public class</jk> Bar {...}
1441       *
1442       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an indeterminate type.</jc>
1443       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1444       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;
1445       *    }
1446       *
1447       *    <jc>// Create a parser and tell it which classes to try to resolve.</jc>
1448       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1449       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1450       *       .dictionaryOn(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
1451       *       .build();
1452       *
1453       *    <jc>// Parse bean.</jc>
1454       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{mySimpleField:{_type:'foo',...}}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1455       * </p>
1456       *
1457       * <p>
1458       * This is functionally equivalent to the {@link Marshalled#dictionary()} annotation.
1459       *
1460       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1461       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#dictionary()}
1462       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beanDictionary(Class...)}
1463       * </ul>
1464       *
1465       * @param on The class that the dictionary values apply to.
1466       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1467       * @param values
1468       *    The new values for this setting.
1469       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
1470       * @return This object.
1471       */
1472      public SELF dictionaryOn(Class<?> on, Class<?>...values) {
1473         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
1474         bcBuilder.dictionaryOn(assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on), values);
1475         return self();
1476      }
1477
1478      /**
1479       * Beans don't require at least one property.
1480       *
1481       * <p>
1482       * When enabled, then a Java class doesn't need to contain at least 1 property to be considered a bean.
1483       * Otherwise, the bean will be serialized as a string using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
1484       *
1485       * <p>
1486       * The {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a class to override this setting when <jk>true</jk>.
1487       *
1488       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1489       * <p class='bjava'>
1490       *    <jc>// A bean with no properties.</jc>
1491       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1492       *    }
1493       *
1494       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that serializes beans even if they have zero properties.</jc>
1495       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1496       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1497       *       .disableBeansRequireSomeProperties()
1498       *       .build();
1499       *
1500       *    <jc>// Produces:  {}</jc>
1501       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1502       * </p>
1503       *
1504       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1505       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotation can be used on the class to force it to be recognized as a bean class
1506       *       even if it has no properties.
1507       * </ul>
1508       *
1509       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1510       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableBeansRequireSomeProperties()}
1511       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#disableBeansRequireSomeProperties()}
1512       * </ul>
1513       *
1514       * @return This object.
1515       */
1516      public SELF disableBeansRequireSomeProperties() {
1517         bcBuilder.disableBeansRequireSomeProperties();
1518         return self();
1519      }
1520
1521      /**
1522       * Don't silently ignore missing setters.
1523       *
1524       * <p>
1525       * When enabled, trying to set a value on a bean property without a setter will throw a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
1526       * Otherwise, it will be silently ignored.
1527       *
1528       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1529       * <p class='bjava'>
1530       *    <jc>// A bean with a property with a getter but not a setter.</jc>
1531       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1532       *       <jk>public void</jk> getFoo() {
1533       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"foo"</js>;
1534       *       }
1535       *    }
1536       *
1537       *    <jc>// Create a parser that throws an exception if a setter is not found but a getter is.</jc>
1538       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1539       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1540       *       .disableIgnoreMissingSetters()
1541       *       .build();
1542       *
1543       *    <jc>// Throws a ParseException.</jc>
1544       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1545       * </p>
1546       *
1547       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1548       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on getters and fields to ignore them.
1549       * </ul>
1550       *
1551       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1552       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableIgnoreMissingSetters()}
1553       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#disableIgnoreMissingSetters()}
1554       * </ul>
1555       *
1556       * @return This object.
1557       */
1558      public SELF disableIgnoreMissingSetters() {
1559         bcBuilder.disableIgnoreMissingSetters();
1560         return self();
1561      }
1562
1563      /**
1564       * Don't ignore transient fields.
1565       *
1566       * <p>
1567       * When enabled, methods and fields marked as <jk>transient</jk> will not be ignored as bean properties.
1568       *
1569       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1570       * <p class='bjava'>
1571       *    <jc>// A bean with a transient field.</jc>
1572       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1573       *       <jk>public transient</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>;
1574       *    }
1575       *
1576       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that doesn't ignore transient fields.</jc>
1577       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1578       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1579       *       .disableIgnoreTransientFields()
1580       *       .build();
1581       *
1582       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
1583       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1584       * </p>
1585       *
1586       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1587       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used on transient fields to keep them from being ignored.
1588       * </ul>
1589       *
1590       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1591       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableIgnoreTransientFields()}
1592       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#disableIgnoreTransientFields()}
1593       * </ul>
1594       *
1595       * @return This object.
1596       */
1597      public SELF disableIgnoreTransientFields() {
1598         bcBuilder.disableIgnoreTransientFields();
1599         return self();
1600      }
1601
1602      /**
1603       * Don't ignore unknown properties with null values.
1604       *
1605       * <p>
1606       * When enabled, trying to set a <jk>null</jk> value on a non-existent bean property will throw a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
1607       * Otherwise it will be silently ignored.
1608       *
1609       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1610       * <p class='bjava'>
1611       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
1612       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1613       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>;
1614       *    }
1615       *
1616       *    <jc>// Create a parser that throws an exception on an unknown property even if the value being set is null.</jc>
1617       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1618       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1619       *       .disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()
1620       *       .build();
1621       *
1622       *    <jc>// Throws a BeanRuntimeException wrapped in a ParseException on the unknown 'bar' property.</jc>
1623       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:null}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1624       * </p>
1625       *
1626       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1627       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()}
1628       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()}
1629       * </ul>
1630       *
1631       * @return This object.
1632       */
1633      public SELF disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties() {
1634         bcBuilder.disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties();
1635         return self();
1636      }
1637
1638      /**
1639       * Don't use interface proxies.
1640       *
1641       * <p>
1642       * When enabled, interfaces will be instantiated as proxy classes through the use of an
1643       * {@link InvocationHandler} if there is no other way of instantiating them.
1644       * Otherwise, throws a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
1645       *
1646       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1647       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableInterfaceProxies()}
1648       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#disableInterfaceProxies()}
1649       * </ul>
1650       *
1651       * @return This object.
1652       */
1653      public SELF disableInterfaceProxies() {
1654         bcBuilder.disableInterfaceProxies();
1655         return self();
1656      }
1657
1658      /**
1659       * Sets the default active view name for serializer/parser sessions created from this context.
1660       *
1661       * <p>
1662       * When set, only bean properties whose declared {@link MarshalledProp#view()} set contains this view name
1663       * will be included during serialization.  On the parse side, out-of-view properties are routed through
1664       * the existing unknown/ignored-property mechanism governed by {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}.
1665       *
1666       * <p>
1667       * Untagged properties follow the default-view-inclusion policy: included under every active view unless
1668       * {@link #disableDefaultViewInclusion()} has been called.
1669       *
1670       * <p>
1671       * This value can be overridden per call via {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#activeView(String)}.
1672       *
1673       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1674       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledProp#view()}
1675       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#activeView(String)}
1676       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#activeView(String)}
1677       * </ul>
1678       *
1679       * @param value The active view name.  Use <jk>null</jk> to disable view filtering.
1680       * @return This object.
1681       * @since 10.0.0
1682       */
1683      public SELF activeView(String value) {
1684         bcBuilder.activeView(value);
1685         return self();
1686      }
1687
1688      /**
1689       * Disables the default-view-inclusion policy.
1690       *
1691       * <p>
1692       * By default, bean properties that carry no {@link MarshalledProp#view()} declaration are included under
1693       * every active view.  Calling this method reverses the policy: untagged properties are <em>excluded</em>
1694       * when any active view is set.
1695       *
1696       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1697       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledProp#view()}
1698       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#disableDefaultViewInclusion()}
1699       * </ul>
1700       *
1701       * @return This object.
1702       * @since 10.0.0
1703       */
1704      public SELF disableDefaultViewInclusion() {
1705         bcBuilder.disableDefaultViewInclusion();
1706         return self();
1707      }
1708
1709      /**
1710       * POJO example.
1711       *
1712       * <p>
1713       * Specifies an example in JSON of the specified class.
1714       *
1715       * <p>
1716       * Examples are used in cases such as POJO examples in Swagger documents.
1717       *
1718       * <p>
1719       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
1720       *
1721       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1722       * <p class='bjava'>
1723       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1724       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1725       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1726       *       .example(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>)
1727       *       .build();
1728       * </p>
1729       *
1730       * <p>
1731       * This is a shorthand method for the following code:
1732       * <p class='bjava'>
1733       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>pojoClass</jv>).example(<jv>json</jv>).build())
1734       * </p>
1735       *
1736       * <p>
