1737       * POJO examples can also be defined on classes via the following:
1738       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1739       *    <li>A static field annotated with {@link Example @Example}.
1740       *    <li>A static method annotated with {@link Example @Example} with zero arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
1741       *    <li>A static method with name <c>example</c> with no arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
1742       * </ul>
1743       *
1744       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1745       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#example()}
1746       * </ul>
1747       *
1748       * @param <T> The POJO class type.
1749       * @param pojoClass The POJO class.
1750       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1751       * @param json The JSON 5 representation of the example.
1752       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty (treated as no example).
1753       * @return This object.
1754       */
1755      public <T> SELF example(Class<T> pojoClass, String json) {
1756         bcBuilder.example(assertArgNotNull(ARG_pojoClass, pojoClass), json);
1757         return self();
1758      }
1759
1760      /**
1761       * POJO example.
1762       *
1763       * <p>
1764       * Specifies an example of the specified class.
1765       *
1766       * <p>
1767       * Examples are used in cases such as POJO examples in Swagger documents.
1768       *
1769       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1770       * <p class='bjava'>
1771       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1772       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1773       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1774       *       .example(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean().setFoo(<js>"foo"</js>).setBar(123))
1775       *       .build();
1776       * </p>
1777       *
1778       * <p>
1779       * This is a shorthand method for the following code:
1780       * <p class='bjava'>
1781       *       <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>pojoClass</jv>).example(Json5.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.toString(<jv>object</jv>)).build())
1782       * </p>
1783       *
1784       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1785       *    <li class='note'>Using this method assumes the serialized form of the object is the same as that produced
1786       *       by the default serializer.  This may not be true based on settings or swaps on the constructed serializer.
1787       * </ul>
1788       *
1789       * <p>
1790       * POJO examples can also be defined on classes via the following:
1791       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1792       *    <li>The {@link Marshalled#example()} annotation on the class itself.
1793       *    <li>A static field annotated with {@link Example @Example}.
1794       *    <li>A static method annotated with {@link Example @Example} with zero arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
1795       *    <li>A static method with name <c>example</c> with no arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
1796       * </ul>
1797       *
1798       * @param <T> The POJO class.
1799       * @param pojoClass The POJO class.
1800       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1801       * @param o
1802       *    An instance of the POJO class used for examples.
1803       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will be serialized as <js>"null"</js>).
1804       * @return This object.
1805       */
1806      public <T> SELF example(Class<T> pojoClass, T o) {
1807         bcBuilder.example(assertArgNotNull(ARG_pojoClass, pojoClass), o);
1808         return self();
1809      }
1810
1811      /**
1812       * Find fluent setters.
1813       *
1814       * <p>
1815       * When enabled, fluent setters are detected on beans during parsing.
1816       *
1817       * <p>
1818       * Fluent setters must have the following attributes:
1819       * <ul>
1820       *    <li>Public.
1821       *    <li>Not static.
1822       *    <li>Take in one parameter.
1823       *    <li>Return the bean itself.
1824       * </ul>
1825       *
1826       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1827       * <p class='bjava'>
1828       *    <jc>// A bean with a fluent setter.</jc>
1829       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1830       *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean foo(String <jv>value</jv>) {...}
1831       *    }
1832       *
1833       *    <jc>// Create a parser that finds fluent setters.</jc>
1834       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1835       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1836       *       .findFluentSetters()
1837       *       .build();
1838       *
1839       *    <jc>// Parse into bean using fluent setter.</jc>
1840       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1841       * </p>
1842       *
1843       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1844       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used on methods to individually identify them as fluent setters.
1845       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#findFluentSetters() @BeanType(findFluentSetters=true)} annotation can also be used on classes to specify to look for fluent setters.
1846       * </ul>
1847       *
1848       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1849       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#findFluentSetters()}
1850       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#findFluentSetters()}
1851       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#findFluentSetters()}
1852       * </ul>
1853       *
1854       * @return This object.
1855       */
1856      public SELF findFluentSetters() {
1857         bcBuilder.findFluentSetters();
1858         return self();
1859      }
1860
1861      /**
1862       * Find fluent setters.
1863       *
1864       * <p>
1865       * Identical to {@link #findFluentSetters()} but enables it on a specific class only.
1866       *
1867       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1868       * <p class='bjava'>
1869       *    <jc>// A bean with a fluent setter.</jc>
1870       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1871       *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean foo(String <jv>value</jv>) {...}
1872       *    }
1873       *
1874       *    <jc>// Create a parser that finds fluent setters.</jc>
1875       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1876       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1877       *       .findFluentSetters(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
1878       *       .build();
1879       *
1880       *    <jc>// Parse into bean using fluent setter.</jc>
1881       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1882       * </p>
1883       *
1884       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1885       *    <li class='note'>This method is functionally equivalent to using the {@link BeanType#findFluentSetters()} annotation.
1886       * </ul>
1887       *
1888       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1889       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#findFluentSetters()}
1890       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#findFluentSetters()}
1891       * </ul>
1892       *
1893       * @param on The class that this applies to.
1894       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1895       * @return This object.
1896       */
1897      public SELF findFluentSetters(Class<?> on) {
1898         bcBuilder.findFluentSetters(assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on));
1899         return self();
1900      }
1901
1902      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
1903      public HashKey hashKey() {
1904         // @formatter:off
1905         return HashKey.of(
1906            super.hashKey(),
1907            bcBuilder.hashKey(),
1908            bc == null ? 0 : bc.getHashKey()
1909         );
1910         // @formatter:on
1911      }
1912
1913      /**
1914       * Ignore invocation errors on getters.
1915       *
1916       * <p>
1917       * When enabled, errors thrown when calling bean getter methods will silently be ignored.
1918       * Otherwise, a {@code BeanRuntimeException} is thrown.
1919       *
1920       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1921       * <p class='bjava'>
1922       *    <jc>// A bean with a property that throws an exception.</jc>
1923       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1924       *       <jk>public</jk> String getFoo() {
1925       *          <jk>throw new</jk> RuntimeException(<js>"foo"</js>);
1926       *       }
1927       *    }
1928       *
1929       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores bean getter exceptions.</jc>
1930       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1931       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1932       *       .ingoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()
1933       *       .build();
1934       *
1935       *    <jc>// Exception is ignored.</jc>
1936       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1937       * </p>
1938       *
1939       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1940       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()}
1941       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()}
1942       * </ul>
1943       *
1944       * @return This object.
1945       */
1946      public SELF ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters() {
1947         bcBuilder.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters();
1948         return self();
1949      }
1950
1951      /**
1952       * Ignore invocation errors on setters.
1953       *
1954       * <p>
1955       * When enabled, errors thrown when calling bean setter methods will silently be ignored.
1956       * Otherwise, a {@code BeanRuntimeException} is thrown.
1957       *
1958       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1959       * <p class='bjava'>
1960       *    <jc>// A bean with a property that throws an exception.</jc>
1961       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1962       *       <jk>public void</jk> setFoo(String <jv>foo</jv>) {
1963       *          <jk>throw new</jk> RuntimeException(<js>"foo"</js>);
1964       *       }
1965       *    }
1966       *
1967       *    <jc>// Create a parser that ignores bean setter exceptions.</jc>
1968       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1969       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1970       *       .ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()
1971       *       .build();
1972       *
1973       *    <jc>// Exception is ignored.</jc>
1974       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1975       * </p>
1976       *
1977       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1978       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()}
1979       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()}
1980       * </ul>
1981       *
1982       * @return This object.
1983       */
1984      public SELF ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters() {
1985         bcBuilder.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters();
1986         return self();
1987      }
1988
1989      /**
1990       * Ignore unknown properties.
1991       *
1992       * <p>
1993       * When enabled, trying to set a value on a non-existent bean property will silently be ignored.
1994       * Otherwise, a {@code BeanRuntimeException} is thrown.
1995       *
1996       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1997       * <p class='bjava'>
1998       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
1999       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2000       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>;
2001       *    }
2002       *
2003       *    <jc>// Create a parser that ignores missing bean properties.</jc>
2004       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2005       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2006       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
2007       *       .build();
2008       *
2009       *    <jc>// Doesn't throw an exception on unknown 'bar' property.</jc>
2010       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2011       * </p>
2012       *
2013       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2014       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
2015       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
2016       * </ul>
2017       *
2018       * @return This object.
2019       */
2020      public SELF ignoreUnknownBeanProperties() {
2021         bcBuilder.ignoreUnknownBeanProperties();
2022         return self();
2023      }
2024
2025      /**
2026       * Schema validation mode.
2027       *
2028       * <p>
2029       * When enabled, bean property values are validated against the constraints declared by
2030       * {@link Schema @Schema} annotations on those properties.  Validation
2031       * runs during both <b>parsing</b> (value set on the bean) and <b>serialization</b> (value read from the bean).
2032       *
2033       * <p>
2034       * Validation is backed by the typed {@code JsonSchema} bean in {@code juneau-bean-jsonschema} and follows
2035       * JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 semantics.  Validation failures throw
2036       * {@link SchemaValidationException} wrapped in
2037       * {@code BeanRuntimeException}; the parser surfaces them as {@link ParseException}
2038       * and the serializer surfaces them as {@link SerializeException}.
2039       *
2040       * <p>
2041       * If {@code juneau-bean-jsonschema} is not on the classpath, this flag becomes a silent no-op.
2042       *
2043       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2044       * <p class='bjava'>
2045       *    <jc>// A bean with a length-constrained property.</jc>
2046       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2047       *       <ja>&#64;Schema</ja>(minLength=<js>"2"</js>, maxLength=<js>"10"</js>)
2048       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>name</jf>;
2049       *    }
2050       *
2051       *    <jc>// Parser that validates inbound values.</jc>
2052       *    ReaderParser <jv>p</jv> = JsonParser.<jsm>create</jsm>().validateSchema().build();
2053       *
2054       *    <jv>p</jv>.parse(<js>"{name:'a'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// throws ParseException - too short</jc>
2055       * </p>
2056       *
2057       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2058       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#validateSchema()}
2059       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#validateSchema()}
2060       * </ul>
2061       *
2062       * @return This object.
2063       * @since 10.0.0
2064       */
2065      public SELF validateSchema() {
2066         bcBuilder.validateSchema();
2067         return self();
2068      }
2069
2070      /**
2071       * Same as {@link #validateSchema()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2072       *
2073       * @param value The value for this setting.
2074       * @return This object.
2075       * @since 10.0.0
2076       */
2077      public SELF validateSchema(boolean value) {
2078         bcBuilder.validateSchema(value);
2079         return self();
2080      }
2081
2082      /**
2083       * Ignore unknown enum values.
2084       *
2085       * <p>
2086       * When enabled, unknown enum values are set to <jk>null</jk> instead of throwing a parse exception.
2087       *
2088       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2089       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreUnknownEnumValues()}
2090       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#ignoreUnknownEnumValues()}
2091       * </ul>
2092       *
2093       * @return This object.
2094       */
2095      public SELF ignoreUnknownEnumValues() {
2096         bcBuilder.ignoreUnknownEnumValues();
2097         return self();
2098      }
2099
2100      /**
2101       * Implementation classes.
2102       *
2103       * <p>
2104       * For interfaces and abstract classes this method can be used to specify an implementation class for the
2105       * interface/abstract class so that instances of the implementation class are used when instantiated (e.g. during a
2106       * parse).
2107       *
2108       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2109       * <p class='bjava'>
2110       *    <jc>// A bean interface.</jc>
2111       *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyBean {
2112       *       ...
2113       *    }
2114       *
2115       *    <jc>// A bean implementation.</jc>
2116       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanImpl <jk>implements</jk> MyBean {
2117       *       ...
2118       *    }
2119
2120       *    <jc>// Create a parser that instantiates MyBeanImpls when parsing MyBeans.</jc>
2121       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2122       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2123       *       .implClass(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, MyBeanImpl.<jk>class</jk>)
2124       *       .build();
2125       *
2126       *    <jc>// Instantiates a MyBeanImpl,</jc>
2127       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"..."</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2128       * </p>
2129       *
2130       * @param interfaceClass The interface class.
2131       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2132       * @param implClass The implementation class.
2133       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2134       * @return This object.
2135       */
2136      public SELF implClass(Class<?> interfaceClass, Class<?> implClass) {
2137         bcBuilder.implClass(assertArgNotNull(ARG_interfaceClass, interfaceClass), assertArgNotNull(ARG_implClass, implClass));
2138         return self();
2139      }
2140
2141      /**
2142       * Implementation classes.
2143       *
2144       * <p>
2145       * For interfaces and abstract classes this method can be used to specify an implementation class for the
2146       * interface/abstract class so that instances of the implementation class are used when instantiated (e.g. during a
2147       * parse).
2148       *
2149       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2150       * <p class='bjava'>
2151       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2152       *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyBean {
2153       *       ...
2154       *    }
2155       *
2156       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2157       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanImpl <jk>implements</jk> MyBean {
2158       *       ...
2159       *    }
2160
2161       *    <jc>// Create a parser that instantiates MyBeanImpls when parsing MyBeans.</jc>
2162       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2163       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2164       *       .implClasses(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, MyBeanImpl.<jk>class</jk>))
2165       *       .build();
2166       *
2167       *    <jc>// Instantiates a MyBeanImpl,</jc>
2168       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"..."</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2169       * </p>
2170       *
2171       * @param values
2172       *    The new value for this setting.
2173       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2174       * @return This object.
2175       */
2176      public SELF implClasses(Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> values) {
2177         bcBuilder.implClasses(assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values));
2178         return self();
2179      }
2180
2181      /**
2182       * Identifies a class to be used as the interface class for the specified class and all subclasses.
2183       *
2184       * <p>
2185       * When specified, only the list of properties defined on the interface class will be used during serialization.
2186       * Additional properties on subclasses will be ignored.
2187       *
2188       * <p class='bjava'>
2189       *    <jc>// Parent class or interface</jc>
2190       *    <jk>public abstract class</jk> A {
2191       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>;
2192       *    }
2193       *
2194       *    <jc>// Sub class</jc>
2195       *    <jk>public class</jk> A1 <jk>extends</jk> A {
2196       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
2197       *    }
2198       *
2199       *    <jc>// Create a serializer and define our interface class mapping.</jc>
2200       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2201       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2202       *       .interfaceClass(A1.<jk>class</jk>, A.<jk>class</jk>)
2203       *       .build();
2204       *
2205       *    <jc>// Produces "{"foo":"foo"}"</jc>
2206       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> A1());
2207       * </p>
2208       *
2209       * <p>
2210       * This annotation can be used on the parent class so that it filters to all child classes, or can be set
2211       * individually on the child classes.
2212       *
2213       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2214       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#interfaceClass() @BeanType(interfaceClass)} annotation is the equivalent annotation-based solution.
2215       * </ul>
2216       *
2217       * @param on The class that the interface class applies to.
2218       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2219       * @param value
2220       *    The new value for this setting.
2221       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2222       * @return This object.
2223       */
2224      public SELF interfaceClass(Class<?> on, Class<?> value) {
2225         bcBuilder.interfaceClass(assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on), assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
2226         return self();
2227      }
2228
2229      /**
2230       * Identifies a set of interfaces.
2231       *
2232       * <p>
2233       * When specified, only the list of properties defined on the interface class will be used during serialization
2234       * of implementation classes.  Additional properties on subclasses will be ignored.
2235       *
2236       * <p class='bjava'>
2237       *    <jc>// Parent class or interface</jc>
2238       *    <jk>public abstract class</jk> A {
2239       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>;
2240       *    }
2241       *
2242       *    <jc>// Sub class</jc>
2243       *    <jk>public class</jk> A1 <jk>extends</jk> A {
2244       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
2245       *    }
2246       *
2247       *    <jc>// Create a serializer and define our interface class mapping.</jc>
2248       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2249       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2250       *       .interfaces(A.<jk>class</jk>)
2251       *       .build();
2252       *
2253       *    <jc>// Produces "{"foo":"foo"}"</jc>
2254       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> A1());
2255       * </p>
2256       *
2257       * <p>
2258       * This annotation can be used on the parent class so that it filters to all child classes, or can be set
2259       * individually on the child classes.
2260       *
2261       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2262       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#interfaceClass() @BeanType(interfaceClass)} annotation is the equivalent annotation-based solution.
2263       * </ul>
2264       *
2265       * @param value
2266       *    The new value for this setting.
2267       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2268       * @return This object.
2269       */
2270      public SELF interfaces(Class<?>...value) {
2271         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_value, value);
2272         bcBuilder.interfaces(value);
2273         return self();
2274      }
2275
2276      /**
2277       * <i><l>Context</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Locale.
2278       *
2279       * <p>
2280       * Specifies the default locale for serializer and parser sessions when not specified via {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#locale(Locale)}.
2281       * Typically used for POJO swaps that need to deal with locales such as swaps that convert <l>Date</l> and <l>Calendar</l>
2282       * objects to strings by accessing it via the session passed into the {@link ObjectSwap#swap(MarshallingSession, Object)} and
2283       * {@link ObjectSwap#unswap(MarshallingSession, Object, ClassMeta, String)} methods.
2284       *
2285       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2286       * <p class='bjava'>
2287       *    <jc>// Define a POJO swap that skips serializing beans if we're in the UK.</jc>
2288       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanSwap <jk>extends</jk> StringSwap&lt;MyBean&gt; {
2289       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2290       *       <jk>public</jk> String swap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2291       *          <jk>if</jk> (<jv>session</jv>.getLocale().equals(Locale.<jsf>UK</jsf>))
2292       *             <jk>return null</jk>;
2293       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>bean</jv>.toString();
2294       *       }
2295       *    }
2296       *
2297       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses the specified locale if it's not passed in through session args.</jc>
2298       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2299       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2300       *       .locale(Locale.<jsf>UK</jsf>)
2301       *       .swaps(MyBeanSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
2302       *       .build();
2303       * </p>
2304       *
2305       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2306       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#locale()}
2307       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#locale(Locale)}
2308       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#locale(Locale)}
2309       * </ul>
2310       *
2311       * @param value The new value for this property.
2312       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2313       * @return This object.
2314       */
2315      public SELF locale(Locale value) {
2316         bcBuilder.locale(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
2317         return self();
2318      }
2319
2320      /**
2321       * <i><l>Context</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Media type.
2322       *
2323       * <p>
2324       * Specifies the default media type for serializer and parser sessions when not specified via {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#mediaType(MediaType)}.
2325       * Typically used for POJO swaps that need to serialize the same POJO classes differently depending on
2326       * the specific requested media type.   For example, a swap could handle a request for media types <js>"application/json"</js>
2327       * and <js>"application/json+foo"</js> slightly differently even though they're both being handled by the same JSON
2328       * serializer or parser.
2329       *
2330       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2331       * <p class='bjava'>
2332       *    <jc>// Define a POJO swap that skips serializing beans if the media type is application/json.</jc>
2333       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanSwap <jk>extends</jk> StringSwap&lt;MyBean&gt; {
2334       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2335       *       <jk>public</jk> String swap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2336       *          <jk>if</jk> (<jv>session</jv>.getMediaType().equals(<js>"application/json"</js>))
2337       *             <jk>return null</jk>;
2338       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>bean</jv>.toString();
2339       *       }
2340       *    }
2341       *
2342       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses the specified media type if it's not passed in through session args.</jc>
2343       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2344       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2345       *       .mediaType(MediaType.<jsf>JSON</jsf>)
2346       *       .build();
2347       * </p>
2348       *
2349       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2350       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#mediaType()}
2351       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#mediaType(MediaType)}
2352       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#mediaType(MediaType)}
2353       * </ul>
2354       *
2355       * @param value The new value for this property.
2356       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no default media type will be set).
2357       * @return This object.
2358       */
2359      public SELF mediaType(MediaType value) {
2360         bcBuilder.mediaType(value);
2361         return self();
2362      }
2363
2364      /**
2365       * Bean class exclusions.
2366       *
2367       * <p>
2368       * List of classes that should not be treated as beans even if they appear to be bean-like.
2369       * Not-bean classes are converted to <c>Strings</c> during serialization.
2370       *
2371       * <p>
2372       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
2373       * <ul>
2374       *    <li>Classes.
2375       *    <li>Arrays and collections of classes.
2376       * </ul>
2377       *
2378       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2379       * <p class='bjava'>
2380       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2381       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2382       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
2383       *
2384       *       <jk>public</jk> String toString() {
2385       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"baz"</js>;
2386       *       }
2387       *    }
2388       *
2389       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that doesn't treat MyBean as a bean class.</jc>
2390       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2391       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2392       *       .notBeanClasses(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
2393       *       .build();
2394       *
2395       *    <jc>// Produces "baz" instead of {"foo":"bar"}</jc>
2396       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2397       * </p>
2398       *
2399       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2400       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on classes to prevent them from being recognized as beans.
2401       * </ul>
2402       *
2403       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2404       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanIgnore}
2405       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#notBeanClasses()}
2406       *    <li class='jf'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#notBeanClasses()}
2407       * </ul>
2408       *
2409       * @param values
2410       *    The values to add to this setting.
2411       *    <br>Values can consist of any of the following types:
2412       *    <ul>
2413       *       <li>Classes.
2414       *       <li>Arrays and collections of classes.
2415       *    </ul>
2416       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2417       * @return This object.
2418       */
2419      public SELF notBeanClasses(Class<?>...values) {
2420         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
2421         bcBuilder.notBeanClasses(values);
2422         return self();
2423      }
2424
2425      /**
2426       * Bean package exclusions.
2427       *
2428       * <p>
2429       * Used as a convenient way of defining the {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#notBeanClasses(Class...)} property for entire packages.
2430       * Any classes within these packages will be serialized to strings using {@link Object#toString()}.
2431       *
2432       * <p>
2433       * Note that you can specify suffix patterns to include all subpackages.
2434       *
2435       * <p>
2436       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
2437       * <ul>
2438       *    <li>Strings.
2439       *    <li>Arrays and collections of strings.
2440       * </ul>
2441       *
2442       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2443       * <p class='bjava'>
2444       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores beans in the specified packages.</jc>
2445       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2446       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2447       *       .notBeanPackages(<js>"org.apache.foo"</js>, <js>"org.apache.bar.*"</js>)
2448       *       .build();
2449       * </p>
2450       *
2451       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2452       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#notBeanPackages(String...)}
2453       * </ul>
2454       *
2455       * @param values
2456       *    The values to add to this setting.
2457       *    <br>Values can consist of any of the following types:
2458       *    <ul>
2459       *       <li>{@link Package} objects.
2460       *       <li>Strings.
2461       *       <li>Arrays and collections of anything in this list.
2462       *    </ul>
2463       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2464       * @return This object.
2465       */
2466      public SELF notBeanPackages(String...values) {
2467         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
2468         bcBuilder.notBeanPackages(values);
2469         return self();
2470      }
2471
2472      /**
2473       * Bean property namer
2474       *
2475       * <p>
2476       * Same as {@link #propertyNamer(Class)} but allows you to specify a namer for a specific class.
2477       *
2478       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2479       * <p class='bjava'>
2480       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2481       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2482       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>fooBarBaz</jf> = <js>"fooBarBaz"</js>;
2483       *    }
2484       *
2485       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses Dashed-Lower-Case property names for the MyBean class only.</jc>
2486       *    <jc>// (e.g. "foo-bar-baz" instead of "fooBarBaz")</jc>
2487       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2488       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2489       *       .propertyNamer(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, PropertyNamerDLC.<jk>class</jk>)
2490       *       .build();
2491       *
2492       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo-bar-baz":"fooBarBaz"}</jc>
2493       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2494       * </p>
2495       *
2496       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2497       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#propertyNamer() @BeanType(propertyNamer)}
2498       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#propertyNamer(Class)}
2499       * </ul>
2500       *
2501       * @param on The class that the namer applies to.
2502       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2503       * @param value
2504       *    The new value for this setting.
2505       *    <br>The default is {@link BasicPropertyNamer}.
2506       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2507       * @return This object.
2508       */
2509      public SELF propertyNamer(Class<?> on, Class<? extends PropertyNamer> value) {
2510         bcBuilder.propertyNamer(assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on), assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
2511         return self();
2512      }
2513
2514      /**
2515       * Bean property namer
2516       *
2517       * <p>
2518       * The class to use for calculating bean property names.
2519       *
2520       * <p>
2521       * Predefined classes:
2522       * <ul>
2523       *    <li>{@link BasicPropertyNamer} - Default.
2524       *    <li>{@link PropertyNamerDLC} - Dashed-lower-case names.
2525       *    <li>{@link PropertyNamerULC} - Dashed-upper-case names.
2526       * </ul>
2527       *
2528       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2529       * <p class='bjava'>
2530       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2531       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2532       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>fooBarBaz</jf> = <js>"fooBarBaz"</js>;
2533       *    }
2534       *
2535       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses Dashed-Lower-Case property names.</jc>
2536       *    <jc>// (e.g. "foo-bar-baz" instead of "fooBarBaz")</jc>
2537       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2538       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2539       *       .propertyNamer(PropertyNamerDLC.<jk>class</jk>)
2540       *       .build();
2541       *
2542       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo-bar-baz":"fooBarBaz"}</jc>
2543       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2544       * </p>
2545       *
2546       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2547       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#propertyNamer(Class)}
2548       * </ul>
2549       *
2550       * @param value
2551       *    The new value for this setting.
2552       *    <br>The default is {@link BasicPropertyNamer}.
2553       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2554       * @return This object.
2555       */
2556      public SELF propertyNamer(Class<? extends PropertyNamer> value) {
2557         bcBuilder.propertyNamer(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
2558         return self();
2559      }
2560
2561      /**
2562       * Opt out of alphabetical property sorting globally.
2563       *
2564       * <p>
2565       * When called, bean properties will be serialized in natural JVM-dependent order instead of the default alphabetical order.
2566       *
2567       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2568       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#unsortedProperties()}
2569       * </ul>
2570       *
2571       * @return This object.
2572       */
2573      public SELF unsortedProperties() {
2574         bcBuilder.unsortedProperties();
2575         return self();
2576      }
2577
2578      /**
2579       * Opt specific bean classes out of alphabetical property sorting.
2580       *
2581       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2582       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#unsorted() @BeanType(unsorted)}
2583       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#unsortedProperties(Class...)}
2584       * </ul>
2585       *
2586       * @param on The bean classes to opt out of sorted properties.
2587       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2588       * @return This object.
2589       */
2590      public SELF unsortedProperties(Class<?>...on) {
2591         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_on, on);
2592         bcBuilder.unsortedProperties(on);
2593         return self();
2594      }
2595
2596      /**
2597       * Identifies a stop class for the annotated class.
2598       *
2599       * <p>
2600       * Identical in purpose to the stop class specified by {@link Introspector#getBeanInfo(Class, Class)}.
2601       * Any properties in the stop class or in its base classes will be ignored during analysis.
2602       *
2603       * <p>
2604       * For example, in the following class hierarchy, instances of <c>C3</c> will include property <c>p3</c>,
2605       * but not <c>p1</c> or <c>p2</c>.
2606       *
2607       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2608       * <p class='bjava'>
2609       *    <jk>public class</jk> C1 {
2610       *       <jk>public int</jk> getP1();
2611       *    }
2612       *
2613       *    <jk>public class</jk> C2 <jk>extends</jk> C1 {
2614       *       <jk>public int</jk> getP2();
2615       *    }
2616       *
2617       *    <jk>public class</jk> C3 <jk>extends</jk> C2 {
2618       *       <jk>public int</jk> getP3();
2619       *    }
2620       *
2621       *    <jc>// Create a serializer specifies a stop class for C3.</jc>
2622       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2623       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2624       *       .stopClass(C3.<jk>class</jk>, C2.<jk>class</jk>)
2625       *       .build();
2626       *
2627       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"p3":"..."}</jc>
2628       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> C3());
2629       * </p>
2630       *
2631       * @param on The class on which the stop class is being applied.
2632       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2633       * @param value
2634       *    The new value for this setting.
2635       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2636       * @return This object.
2637       */
2638      public SELF stopClass(Class<?> on, Class<?> value) {
2639         bcBuilder.stopClass(assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on), assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
2640         return self();
2641      }
2642
2643      /**
2644       * A shortcut for defining a {@link FunctionalSwap}.
2645       *
2646       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2647       * <p class='bjava'>
2648       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that performs a custom format for Date objects.</jc>
2649       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2650       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2651       *       .swap(Date.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jsm>format</jsm>(<jv>x</jv>))
2652       *       .build();
2653       * </p>
2654       *
2655       * @param <T> The object type being swapped out.
2656       * @param <S> The object type being swapped in.
2657       * @param normalClass The object type being swapped out.
2658       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2659       * @param swappedClass The object type being swapped in.
2660       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2661       * @param swapFunction The function to convert the object.
2662       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2663       * @return This object.
2664       */
2665      public <T,S> SELF swap(Class<T> normalClass, Class<S> swappedClass, ThrowingFunction<T,S> swapFunction) {
2666         bcBuilder.swap(assertArgNotNull(ARG_normalClass, normalClass), assertArgNotNull(ARG_swappedClass, swappedClass), assertArgNotNull(ARG_swapFunction, swapFunction));
2667         return self();
2668      }
2669
2670      /**
2671       * A shortcut for defining a {@link FunctionalSwap}.
2672       *
2673       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2674       * <p class='bjava'>
2675       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that performs a custom format for Date objects.</jc>
2676       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2677       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2678       *       .swap(Date.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jsm>format</jsm>(<jv>x</jv>), <jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jsm>parse</jsm>(<jv>x</jv>))
2679       *       .build();
2680       * </p>
2681       *
2682       * @param <T> The object type being swapped out.
2683       * @param <S> The object type being swapped in.
2684       * @param normalClass The object type being swapped out.
2685       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2686       * @param swappedClass The object type being swapped in.
2687       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2688       * @param swapFunction The function to convert the object during serialization.
2689       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2690       * @param unswapFunction The function to convert the object during parsing.
2691       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2692       * @return This object.
2693       */
2694      public <T,S> SELF swap(Class<T> normalClass, Class<S> swappedClass, ThrowingFunction<T,S> swapFunction, ThrowingFunction<S,T> unswapFunction) {
2695         bcBuilder.swap(assertArgNotNull(ARG_normalClass, normalClass), assertArgNotNull(ARG_swappedClass, swappedClass), assertArgNotNull(ARG_swapFunction, swapFunction), assertArgNotNull(ARG_unswapFunction, unswapFunction));
2696         return self();
2697      }
2698
2699      /**
2700       * Same as {@link #swaps(Object...)} except explicitly specifies class varargs to avoid compilation warnings.
2701       *
2702       * @param values
2703       *    The values to add to this setting.
2704       *    <br>Values can consist of any of the following types:
2705       *    <ul>
2706       *       <li>Any subclass of {@link ObjectSwap}.
2707       *       <li>Any surrogate class.  A shortcut for defining a {@link SurrogateSwap}.
2708       *    </ul>
2709       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2710       * @return This object.
2711       */
2712      public SELF swaps(Class<?>...values) {
2713         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
2714         bcBuilder.swaps(values);
2715         return self();
2716      }
2717
2718      /**
2719       * Java object swaps.
2720       *
2721       * <p>
2722       * Swaps are used to "swap out" non-serializable classes with serializable equivalents during serialization,
2723       * and "swap in" the non-serializable class during parsing.
2724       *
2725       * <p>
2726       * An example of a swap would be a <c>Calendar</c> object that gets swapped out for an ISO8601 string.
2727       *
2728       * <p>
2729       * Multiple swaps can be associated with a single class.
2730       * When multiple swaps are applicable to the same class, the media type pattern defined by
2731       * {@link ObjectSwap#forMediaTypes()} or {@link Swap#mediaTypes() @Swap(mediaTypes)} are used to come up with the best match.
2732       *
2733       * <p>
2734       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
2735       * <ul>
2736       *    <li>Any subclass of {@link ObjectSwap}.
2737       *    <li>Any instance of {@link ObjectSwap}.
2738       *    <li>Any surrogate class.  A shortcut for defining a {@link SurrogateSwap}.
2739       *    <li>Any array or collection of the objects above.
2740       * </ul>
2741       *
2742       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2743       * <p class='bjava'>
2744       *    <jc>// Sample swap for converting Dates to ISO8601 strings.</jc>
2745       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyDateSwap <jk>extends</jk> StringSwap&lt;Date&gt; {
2746       *       <jc>// ISO8601 formatter.</jc>
2747       *       <jk>private</jk> DateFormat <jf>format</jf> = <jk>new</jk> SimpleDateFormat(<js>"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ"</js>);
2748       *
2749       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2750       *       <jk>public</jk> String swap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, Date <jv>date</jv>) {
2751       *          <jk>return</jk> <jf>format</jf>.format(<jv>date</jv>);
2752       *       }
2753       *
2754       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2755       *       <jk>public</jk> Date unswap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, String <jv>string</jv>, ClassMeta <jv>hint</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2756       *          <jk>return</jk> <jf>format</jf>.parse(<jv>string</jv>);
2757       *       }
2758       *    }
2759       *
2760       *    <jc>// Sample bean with a Date field.</jc>
2761       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2762       *       <jk>public</jk> Date <jf>date</jf> = <jk>new</jk> Date(112, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6);
2763       *    }
2764       *
2765       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses our date swap.</jc>
2766       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2767       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2768       *       .swaps(MyDateSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
2769       *       .build();
2770       *
2771       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"date":"2012-03-03T04:05:06-0500"}</jc>
2772       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2773       *
2774       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses our date swap.</jc>
2775       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2776       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2777       *       .swaps(MyDateSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
2778       *       .build();
2779       *
2780       *    <jc>// Use our parser to parse a bean.</jc>
2781       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2782       * </p>
2783       *
2784       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2785       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Swap @Swap} annotation can also be used on classes to identify swaps for the class.
2786       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Swap @Swap} annotation can also be used on bean methods and fields to identify swaps for values of those bean properties.
2787       * </ul>
2788       *
2789       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2790       *    <li class='jf'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#swaps(Class...)}
2791       * </ul>
2792       *
2793       * @param values
2794       *    The values to add to this setting.
2795       *    <br>Values can consist of any of the following types:
2796       *    <ul>
2797       *       <li>Any subclass of {@link ObjectSwap}.
2798       *       <li>Any surrogate class.  A shortcut for defining a {@link SurrogateSwap}.
2799       *       <li>Any array/collection/stream of the objects above.
2800       *    </ul>
2801       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2802       * @return This object.
2803       */
2804      public SELF swaps(Object...values) {
2805         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
2806         bcBuilder.swaps(values);
2807         return self();
2808      }
2809
2810      /**
2811       * <i><l>Context</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  TimeZone.
2812       *
2813       * <p>
2814       * Specifies the default time zone for serializer and parser sessions when not specified via {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#timeZone(TimeZone)}.
2815       * Typically used for POJO swaps that need to deal with timezones such as swaps that convert <l>Date</l> and <l>Calendar</l>
2816       * objects to strings by accessing it via the session passed into the {@link ObjectSwap#swap(MarshallingSession, Object)} and
2817       * {@link ObjectSwap#unswap(MarshallingSession, Object, ClassMeta, String)} methods.
2818       *
2819       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2820       * <p class='bjava'>
2821       *    <jc>// Define a POJO swap that skips serializing beans if the time zone is GMT.</jc>
2822       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanSwap <jk>extends</jk> StringSwap&lt;MyBean&gt; {
2823       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2824       *       <jk>public</jk> String swap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2825       *          <jk>if</jk> (<jv>session</jv>.getTimeZone().equals(TimeZone.<jsf>GMT</jsf>))
2826       *             <jk>return null</jk>;
2827       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>bean</jv>.toString();
2828       *       }
2829       *    }
2830       *
2831       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses GMT if the timezone is not specified in the session args.</jc>
2832       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2833       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2834       *       .timeZone(TimeZone.<jsf>GMT</jsf>)
2835       *       .build();
2836       * </p>
2837       *
2838       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2839       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#timeZone()}
2840       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#timeZone(TimeZone)}
2841       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#timeZone(TimeZone)}
2842       * </ul>
2843       *
2844       * @param value The new value for this property.
2845       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (timezone will not be set, defaults to system timezone).
2846       * @return This object.
2847       */
2848      public SELF timeZone(TimeZone value) {
2849         bcBuilder.timeZone(value);
2850         return self();
2851      }
2852
2853      /**
2854       * Duration wire format.
2855       *
2856       * @param value The new value for this property.
2857       * @return This object.
2858       */
2859      public SELF durationFormat(DurationFormat value) {
2860         bcBuilder.durationFormat(value);
2861         return self();
2862      }
2863
2864      /**
2865       * Period wire format.
2866       *
2867       * @param value The new value for this property.
2868       * @return This object.
2869       */
2870      public SELF periodFormat(PeriodFormat value) {
2871         bcBuilder.periodFormat(value);
2872         return self();
2873      }
2874
2875      /**
2876       * Calendar wire format.
2877       *
2878       * @param value The new value for this property.
2879       * @return This object.
2880       */
2881      public SELF calendarFormat(CalendarFormat value) {
2882         bcBuilder.calendarFormat(value);
2883         return self();
2884      }
2885
2886      /**
2887       * Date wire format.
2888       *
2889       * @param value The new value for this property.
2890       * @return This object.
2891       */
2892      public SELF dateFormat(DateFormat value) {
2893         bcBuilder.dateFormat(value);
2894         return self();
2895      }
2896
2897      /**
2898       * Temporal wire format.
2899       *
2900       * @param value The new value for this property.
2901       * @return This object.
2902       */
2903      public SELF temporalFormat(TemporalFormat value) {
2904         bcBuilder.temporalFormat(value);
2905         return self();
2906      }
2907
2908      /**
2909       * Time-zone wire format.
2910       *
2911       * @param value The new value for this property.
2912       * @return This object.
2913       */
2914      public SELF timeZoneFormat(TimeZoneFormat value) {
2915         bcBuilder.timeZoneFormat(value);
2916         return self();
2917      }
2918
2919      /**
2920       * Locale wire format.
2921       *
2922       * @param value The new value for this property.
2923       * @return This object.
2924       */
2925      public SELF localeFormat(LocaleFormat value) {
2926         bcBuilder.localeFormat(value);
2927         return self();
2928      }
2929
2930      /**
2931       * Binary wire format.
2932       *
2933       * @param value The new value for this property.
2934       * @return This object.
2935       */
2936      public SELF binaryFormat(BinaryFormat value) {
2937         bcBuilder.binaryFormat(value);
2938         return self();
2939      }
2940
2941      /**
2942       * Enum wire format.
2943       *
2944       * @param value The new value for this property.
2945       * @return This object.
2946       */
2947      public SELF enumFormat(EnumFormat value) {
2948         bcBuilder.enumFormat(value);
2949         return self();
2950      }
2951
2952      /**
2953       * UUID wire format.
2954       *
2955       * @param value The new value for this property.
2956       * @return This object.
2957       */
2958      public SELF uuidFormat(UuidFormat value) {
2959         bcBuilder.uuidFormat(value);
2960         return self();
2961      }
2962
2963      /**
2964       * BitSet wire format.
2965       *
2966       * @param value The new value for this property.
2967       * @return This object.
2968       */
2969      public SELF bitSetFormat(BitSetFormat value) {
2970         bcBuilder.bitSetFormat(value);
2971         return self();
2972      }
2973
2974      /**
2975       * Big-number wire format.
2976       *
2977       * @param value The new value for this property.
2978       * @return This object.
2979       */
2980      public SELF bigNumberFormat(BigNumberFormat value) {
2981         bcBuilder.bigNumberFormat(value);
2982         return self();
2983      }
2984
2985      /**
2986       * Boolean wire format.
2987       *
2988       * @param value The new value for this property.
2989       * @return This object.
2990       */
2991      public SELF booleanFormat(BooleanFormat value) {
2992         bcBuilder.booleanFormat(value);
2993         return self();
2994      }
2995
2996      /**
2997       * Float / Double non-finite wire format.
2998       *
2999       * @param value The new value for this property.
3000       * @return This object.
3001       */
3002      public SELF floatFormat(FloatFormat value) {
3003         bcBuilder.floatFormat(value);
3004         return self();
3005      }
3006
3007      /**
3008       * Currency wire format.
3009       *
3010       * @param value The new value for this property.
3011       * @return This object.
3012       */
3013      public SELF currencyFormat(CurrencyFormat value) {
3014         bcBuilder.currencyFormat(value);
3015         return self();
3016      }
3017
3018      /**
3019       * Class wire format.
3020       *
3021       * @param value The new value for this property.
3022       * @return This object.
3023       */
3024      public SELF classFormat(ClassFormat value) {
3025         bcBuilder.classFormat(value);
3026         return self();
3027      }
3028
3029      /**
3030       * Session classloader.
3031       *
3032       * @param value The new value for this property.
3033       * @return This object.
3034       */
3035      public SELF classLoader(ClassLoader value) {
3036         bcBuilder.classLoader(value);
3037         return self();
3038      }
3039
3040      /**
3041       * An identifying name for this class.
3042       *
3043       * <p>
3044       * The name is used to identify the class type during parsing when it cannot be inferred through reflection.
3045       * For example, if a bean property is of type <c>Object</c>, then the serializer will add the name to the
3046       * output so that the class can be determined during parsing.
3047       *
3048       * <p>
3049       * It is also used to specify element names in XML.
3050       *
3051       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3052       * <p class='bjava'>
3053       *    <jc>// Use _type='mybean' to identify this bean.</jc>
3054       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {...}
3055       *
3056       *    <jc>// Create a serializer and specify the type name..</jc>
3057       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3058       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3059       *       .typeName(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"mybean"</js>)
3060       *       .build();
3061       *
3062       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"_type":"mybean",...}</jc>
3063       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
3064       * </p>
3065       *
3066       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3067       *    <li class='note'>Equivalent to the {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)} annotation.
3068       * </ul>
3069       *
3070       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3071       *    <li class='jc'>{@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)}
3072       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beanDictionary(Class...)}
3073       * </ul>
3074       *
3075       * @param on
3076       *    The class the type name is being defined on.
3077       * @param value
3078       *    The new value for this setting.
3079       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3080       * @return This object.
3081       */
3082      public SELF typeName(Class<?> on, String value) {
3083         bcBuilder.typeName(on, assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
3084         return self();
3085      }
3086
3087      /**
3088       * Bean type property name.
3089       *
3090       * <p>
3091       * Same as {@link #typePropertyName(String)} except targets a specific bean class instead of globally.
3092       *
3093       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3094       * <p class='bjava'>
3095       *    <jc>// POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations.</jc>
3096       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"foo"</js>)
3097       *    <jk>public class</jk> Foo {...}
3098       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"bar"</js>)
3099       *    <jk>public class</jk> Bar {...}
3100       *
3101       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an indeterminate type.</jc>
3102       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
3103       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;
3104       *    }
3105       *
3106       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses 't' instead of '_type' for dictionary names.</jc>
3107       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3108       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3109       *       .typePropertyName(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"t"</js>)
3110       *       .dictionary(Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
3111       *       .build();
3112       *
3113       *    <jc>// Produces "{mySimpleField:{t:'foo',...}}".</jc>
3114       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
3115       * </p>
3116       *
3117       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3118       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#typePropertyName() @Marshalled(typePropertyName)}
3119       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#typePropertyName(String)}
3120       * </ul>
3121       *
3122       * @param on The class the type property name applies to.
3123       * @param value
3124       *    The new value for this setting.
3125       *    <br>The default is <js>"_type"</js>.
3126       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3127       * @return This object.
3128       */
3129      public SELF typePropertyName(Class<?> on, String value) {
3130         bcBuilder.typePropertyName(on, assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
3131         return self();
3132      }
3133
3134      /**
3135       * Bean type property name.
3136       *
3137       * <p>
3138       * This specifies the name of the bean property used to store the dictionary name of a bean type so that the
3139       * parser knows the data type to reconstruct.
3140       *
3141       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3142       * <p class='bjava'>
3143       *    <jc>// POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations.</jc>
3144       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"foo"</js>)
3145       *    <jk>public class</jk> Foo {...}
3146       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"bar"</js>)
3147       *    <jk>public class</jk> Bar {...}
3148       *
3149       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses 't' instead of '_type' for dictionary names.</jc>
3150       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3151       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3152       *       .typePropertyName(<js>"t"</js>)
3153       *       .dictionary(Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
3154       *       .build();
3155       *
3156       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses 't' instead of '_type' for dictionary names.</jc>
3157       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
3158       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3159       *       .typePropertyName(<js>"t"</js>)
3160       *       .dictionary(Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
3161       *       .build();
3162       *
3163       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an indeterminate type.</jc>
3164       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
3165       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;
3166       *    }
3167       *
3168       *    <jc>// Produces "{mySimpleField:{t:'foo',...}}".</jc>
3169       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
3170       *
3171       *    <jc>// Parse bean.</jc>
3172       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
3173       * </p>
3174       *
3175       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3176       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#typePropertyName()}
3177       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#typePropertyName()}
3178       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#typePropertyName(String)}
3179       * </ul>
3180       *
3181       * @param value
3182       *    The new value for this setting.
3183       *    <br>The default is <js>"_type"</js>.
3184       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3185       * @return This object.
3186       */
3187      public SELF typePropertyName(String value) {
3188         bcBuilder.typePropertyName(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
3189         return self();
3190      }
3191
3192      /**
3193       * Use Java Introspector.
3194       *
3195       * <p>
3196       * Using the built-in Java bean introspector will not pick up fields or non-standard getters/setters.
3197       * <br>Most {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotations will be ignored.
3198       *
3199       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3200       * <p class='bjava'>
3201       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that only uses the built-in java bean introspector for finding properties.</jc>
3202       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3203       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3204       *       .useJavaBeanIntrospector()
3205       *       .build();
3206       * </p>
3207       *
3208       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3209       *    <li class='jmf'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#useJavaBeanIntrospector()}
3210       * </ul>
3211       *
3212       * @return This object.
3213       */
3214      public SELF useJavaBeanIntrospector() {
3215         bcBuilder.useJavaBeanIntrospector();
3216         return self();
3217      }
3218   }
3219
3220   protected final MarshallingContext marshallingContext;
3221
3222   /**
3223    * Constructor.
3224    *
3225    * @param b The builder for this object.
3226    */
3227   protected MarshallingContextable(Builder b) {
3228      super(b);
3229      marshallingContext = nn(b.bc) ? b.bc : b.bcBuilder.build();
3230   }
3231
3232   /**
3233    * Returns the bean context for this object.
3234    *
3235    * @return The bean context for this object.
3236    */
3237   public MarshallingContext getMarshallingContext() { return marshallingContext; }
3238
3239   /**
3240    * Duration wire format.
3241    *
3242    * @return The configured duration wire format.
3243    */
3244   protected final DurationFormat getDurationFormat() { return marshallingContext.getDurationFormat(); }
3245
3246   /**
3247    * Period wire format.
3248    *
3249    * @return The configured period wire format.
3250    */
3251   protected final PeriodFormat getPeriodFormat() { return marshallingContext.getPeriodFormat(); }
3252
3253   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
3254   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
3255      return super.properties()
3256         .a(PROP_beanContext, marshallingContext.properties());
3257   }
3258}
